findings.evidence: packages/fleury/lib/src/foundation/change_notifier.dart:47-52 — `for (final listener in List<VoidCallback>.of(_listeners)) { listener(); }` snapshots before iterating; removeListener (:39-41) only mutates _listeners, never the in-flight copy, and dispose (:57-60) clears _listeners without stopping an in-flight iteration. The class doc says it 'Mirrors package:flutter/foundation.dart's ChangeNotifier', whose documented contract is that removed listeners are not called after removal within the same notification.
also: lens-error-paths: ChangeNotifier.notifyListeners does not isolate listener exceptions — one throwing listener permanently starves all later-registered listeners (Flutter isolates each)

v1.why: Reproduced at HEAD: change_notifier.dart:49 iterates List.of(_listeners) snapshot; removeListener (:39-41) mutates only _listeners, so a listener removed by an earlier listener during the same notifyListeners still ran (repro printed bCalled=true). Flutter's ChangeNotifier nulls entries in the live list so removed-during-notify listeners are never called; the class doc (:20) claims to 'mirror' Flutter's ChangeNotifier with only the debug assertions omitted, so ported remove-during-notify/dispose-during-notify code can execute callbacks against disposed state. Dart-idiomatic snapshot behavior and the local doc (:43-45) partially describe it, but the mirror claim makes it a real contract deviation. Edge case -> P3.
v1.fix_sketch: Adopt Flutter's scheme: iterate _listeners by index up to a pre-notification length; removeListener during notification nulls the slot instead of removing; compact after the pass. Optionally wrap each call in try/catch so one throwing listener doesn't starve the rest.

findings.title: ListView under unbounded height silently renders nothing instead of failing loudly
findings.failure_scenario: ScrollView(child: Column(mainAxisSize: min, children: [header, ListView(children: [...5 items]), footer])) — the ScrollView measures its child with unbounded rows, so the ListView receives maxRows == null and its performLayout takes the empty path: the rendered frame shows 'header' and 'footer' with all list items silently missing (confirmed empirically). No exception, no dev-mode diagnostic — content just vanishes, and the same silent blank hits the lazy form. The framework's own convention elsewhere is loud failure for un-layoutable configurations (Scrollbar throws on unbounded width, scrollbar.dart:163; LayoutBuilder asserts on unbounded-axis collapse, layout_builder.dart:257), and Flutter throws for a viewport with unbounded main axis, so users porting layouts get silent data loss instead of an actionable error.
findings.evidence: Eager path list_view.dart:870-877: `if (count == 0 || maxRows == null || maxRows == 0)` clears visible children and returns without any diagnostic; lazy path :1274-1279 identical. Confirmed by rendering probe at HEAD: frame contains 'header'/'footer', `contains('item 0') == false`.

v1.why: Traced: eager path list_view.dart:870-877 and lazy path :1274-1279 treat maxRows==null identically to count==0 — clear children, return, no diagnostic. ScrollView measures its child with an unbounded main axis (scroll_view.dart:17, :371-383), so the nested-ListView scenario is reachable and silently drops all items. Framework convention elsewhere is loud failure: Scrollbar throws StateError on unbounded width (scrollbar.dart:161-168; commit cba1f48 fixed exactly this blank-vs-throw for Scrollbar), LayoutBuilder throws on unbounded-axis collapse (layout_builder.dart:~257). Nothing documents empty-on-unbounded as intended; no test pins it.
v1.fix_sketch: In both performLayout empty-branches, split the maxRows==null case out and throw a StateError/FleuryError mirroring Scrollbar's message ('ListView needs a bounded height; give it Expanded/SizedBox or use shrink-wrap'), keeping count==0/maxRows==0 as the legitimate empty render.

findings.title: FrameBuilder interval change recreates its ticker without re-applying TickerMode muting, resuming rebuilds inside a muted subtree
findings.failure_scenario: A spinner/marquee wrapped in TickerMode(enabled: false) (hidden tab, background pane) whose FrameBuilder.interval property changes on a parent rebuild: didUpdateWidget takes the interval branch, disposes the muted ticker and creates a fresh one — FrameTicker._muted defaults to false — then returns. The muted flag is only re-derived in didChangeDependencies, which does not run for a plain widget update, so the supposedly-muted subtree starts firing _onFrame -> setState rebuilds every interval (and ignores AnimationPolicy.disabled) until an ancestor TickerMode/binding dependency actually changes. Hidden panes burn CPU and generate frames the muting lever was specifically added to prevent.
findings.evidence: frame_builder.dart:86-94: `if (widget.interval != oldWidget.interval) { _disposeTicker(); _ensureTicker(); return; }` — _ensureTicker (:67-75) sets only interval/scheduler/listener and start(), never muted; muting is applied solely in didChangeDependencies (:107-116). frame_ticker.dart:58 `bool _muted = false;` confirms the fresh ticker is unmuted, and :172 shows _muted is what gates the user callback.

v1.why: Traced: interval branch (frame_builder.dart:86-94) disposes and recreates via _ensureTicker (:67-75) which sets only interval/scheduler/listener and start(); FrameTicker._muted defaults false (frame_ticker.dart:58) and gates notifyListeners (:172). muted is assigned solely in didChangeDependencies (frame_builder.dart:113-115), which the framework fires only when _dependenciesChanged is set — mount, activate, or inherited notify (framework.dart:1102-1110, :2178-2185); StatefulElement.update -> didUpdateWidget does not set it, and TickerMode.updateShouldNotify is false when enabled is unchanged (ticker_mode.dart:42). So the fresh ticker runs unmuted (and ignores AnimationPolicy.disabled) inside the muted subtree until a real dependency change.
v1.fix_sketch: Re-derive muting whenever the ticker is (re)created: extract the muted computation from didChangeDependencies into a helper and call it at the end of _ensureTicker (or in the interval branch after _ensureTicker), e.g. `_ticker!.muted = !TickerMode.enabledOf(context) || TuiBinding.of(context).animationPolicy == AnimationPolicy.disabled;`.

findings.title: Changing TextInput.blinkInterval/enableBlink while focused kills the blink ticker without recreating it — the cursor can freeze invisible on a focused field
findings.failure_scenario: A focused, blinking TextInput is rebuilt with a different blinkInterval (or enableBlink toggled) while the blink is in its OFF phase — e.g. a settings/theme change flowing new props. didUpdateWidget disposes the ticker and the comment claims it is recreated 'next didChangeDependencies / build pass', but build never recreates it and didChangeDependencies only fires on a dependency notification (a focus change). Verified at HEAD: after the interval change the cursor stayed invisible (cell style inverse=false) across 4 seconds of pumping while node.hasFocus remained true — a focused field with no cursor until the user types (controller change resets _blinkOn) or focus moves.
findings.evidence: text_input.dart 772-776: `if (widget.blinkInterval != oldWidget.blinkInterval || widget.enableBlink != oldWidget.enableBlink) { _disposeBlinkTicker(); }` with no _syncBlinkToFocus()/_ensureBlinkTicker() call; _syncBlinkToFocus is invoked only from didChangeDependencies (780-787), which fires on dependency notifications, not widget updates; build() (1444-1449) only reads _blinkOn. _disposeBlinkTicker leaves _blinkOn at its last phase, so an OFF-phase disposal renders cursorVisible=false indefinitely.

v1.why: text_input.dart:772-776 disposes the ticker with no recreate; _syncBlinkToFocus is called only from didChangeDependencies (786), which the framework fires only when _dependenciesChanged is set (framework.dart:1108-1111) - at init (313), activate (1128), or inherited-notify (2183) - never from StatefulElement.update (1092-1098), so a parent rebuild flowing a new blinkInterval never recreates it. _disposeBlinkTicker (828-834) keeps _blinkOn at its last phase and build's cursorVisible = focused && (!enableBlink || _blinkOn) (1449), so an OFF-phase disposal leaves a focused field cursorless until _onControllerChange (849) or a focus/inherited notification.
v1.fix_sketch: Call _syncBlinkToFocus() right after _disposeBlinkTicker() in didUpdateWidget (context is valid there), which re-creates the ticker at the new interval and normalizes _blinkOn; alternatively reset _blinkOn = true on disposal so the cursor at least stays solid.

findings.title: Clipped scratch blits re-write wide pairs across the clip edge: a CJK glyph at the last clipped column spills its continuation one column past the box, evicting sibling content
findings.failure_scenario: `RenderClip(widthFactor: 0.5)` over '你你你' (6 cols) reports size 3x1 (the expand/collapse animation primitive; same code shape in RenderCellEffect and RenderFlex._paintClipped for overflow clips). The blit loop iterates scratch cols 0..2 and copies leading cells via `buffer.writeGrapheme`; the leading '你' at clipped col 2 is re-written as a full wide pair, placing a continuation cell at col 3 — outside the reported 3-col box. Confirmed by repro at HEAD: a sibling 'X' pre-painted at col 3 is destroyed (evicted to a continuation cell); with the opposite paint order, the sibling's later write evicts the clip-edge glyph instead. Either way one column of adjacent content is corrupted whenever a wide glyph straddles a clip/effect edge — visible during expand/collapse/slide animations over CJK/emoji content, and persistently for any static partial clip.
findings.evidence: render_effect.dart:238-251 (RenderClip): the copy loop bounds cols by `clipped.cols` but `buffer.writeGrapheme(CellOffset(tc,tr), cell.grapheme!)` re-derives width 2 and writes leading+continuation, so a pair straddling the edge writes one column past `clipped.cols`; `_evictWideNeighbors` then clears whatever sibling content was at that column. Identical pattern at render_flex.dart:521-527 (`_paintClipped`) and render_effect.dart:131-148 (RenderCellEffect composite). Contrast with error_presentation.dart:196-221 `_paintClippedRun`, which deliberately drops wide graphemes that do not fully fit ('wide graphemes are dropped, never split'). Repro run at HEAD printed: clip size 3x1, col 2 leading '你', col 3 continuation (was 'X').

v1.why: Reproduced at HEAD. RenderClip blit loop bounds source cols by clipped.cols but buffer.writeGrapheme re-derives width 2 and writes leading+continuation (cell_buffer.dart:430-440), so a '你' at clipped col 2 of a 3-col box writes a continuation at col 3 — outside the reported size; repro showed a pre-painted sibling 'X' at col 3 replaced by Cell.continuation (render_effect.dart:238-251). Same pattern at render_flex.dart:513-527 and RenderCellEffect (render_effect.dart:131-149). error_presentation.dart's _paintClippedRun deliberately drops straddling wide graphemes, confirming intended semantics. One column of corruption only when a wide glyph straddles a clip/effect edge: P3.
v1.fix_sketch: In the scratch-blit loops (RenderClip, RenderCellEffect, RenderFlex._paintClipped), skip a leading cell whose width would extend past the clip edge (mirror error_presentation's 'wide graphemes are dropped, never split'), e.g. check resolved width against remaining cols before writeGrapheme.

findings.title: ESC that aborts an in-progress CSI is swallowed instead of restarting a sequence, turning the immediately following valid escape report into typed text
findings.failure_scenario: Bytes ESC [ ESC [ A arrive in one read — e.g. the user presses Alt+[ and an arrow key within the 30 ms flush debounce, or a mouse/key report loses bytes on a flaky SSH/serial link so a truncated CSI abuts the next report. In _consumeCsi the 0x1B is neither a parameter nor a final byte, so the 'unknown intermediate — abort' branch (input_parser.dart:401-403) resets to ground and DISCARDS the ESC rather than reprocessing it (as ECMA-48/VT parsers do, restarting a sequence at ESC). The following '[' and 'A' are then consumed in ground state as printable input: harness-verified output is TextInputEvent("[") + TextInputEvent("A") — garbage typed into the focused TextField — instead of an arrowUp KeyEvent.
findings.evidence: input_parser.dart:400-403: the CSI abort path is `_resetCsi(); _state = _State.ground;` with no re-consume of the aborting byte, while the analogous UTF-8 bail-out (input_parser.dart:807-813) correctly re-processes the offending byte from ground, and _consumeDiscardedCsi (line 409-417) also correctly special-cases ESC → afterEsc. Harness at HEAD: feed('\x1B[\x1B[A') yields TextInputEvent("["), TextInputEvent("A"). One-line fix shape: treat 0x1B in _consumeCsi like _consumeDiscardedCsi does (reset then _state = afterEsc).

v1.why: input_parser.dart:314-403: in _consumeCsi, 0x1B matches no branch (not 0x30-0x3B params, not 0x3C-0x3F markers, not 0x40-0x7E final) and falls into the abort at 401-403 (_resetCsi; state=ground) which drops the ESC. The following '[' (0x5B) and 'A' then parse in ground as printables -> TextInputEvent (267-270), instead of ESC[A -> arrowUp. _consumeDiscardedCsi (409-413) handles the identical byte correctly (ESC -> afterEsc), and flush() (154-159) only rescues sequences split by the ~30ms idle debounce, so bytes arriving in one read (Alt+[ then arrow, or a truncated CSI abutting the next report on a lossy link) hit the bug. Rare trigger, minor consequence.
v1.fix_sketch: In the _consumeCsi abort branch, special-case 0x1B exactly as _consumeDiscardedCsi does: _resetCsi(); _state = _State.afterEsc; return; (leave the existing reset-to-ground for other unknown bytes).

findings.title: Uncaught async errors thrown after app exit are silently discarded — no stderr log, no crash, no trace (guarded zone + disposed reporter)
findings.failure_scenario: App code schedules async work that outlives the session — a Timer, socket callback, or file-IO continuation created inside runApp's guarded zone (e.g. `Timer(5s, () => throw ...)` from initState). User quits normally (Ctrl+C / requestExit); cleanup disposes the RuntimeErrorReporter and runApp resolves. When the late callback fires and throws, the error is delivered to the still-active `runZonedGuarded` handler: `errorReporter.report(error, stack)` returns immediately without logging (disposed guard runs before `onLog`, runtime_error_overlay.dart:74-76), `reportingFailed` stays false, and the fatal classification is false (stale non-null `frameDriver.rootElement`), so the handler does nothing and `done` is already completed. The error vanishes completely — in plain Dart the same throw would print an unhandled-error trace and terminate with a nonzero exit code. Developers debugging 'my app misbehaves during/after shutdown' get zero signal. Repro confirms: a Timer throwing 300ms after a clean Ctrl+C exit produces no output at all and the process exits 0.
findings.evidence: run_app.dart:1268-1306 — the zone handler's only reporting channel is `errorReporter.report` (:1277), which runtime_error_overlay.dart:74 turns into a silent no-op after `dispose()` (cleanup disposes it at run_app.dart:765); the non-fatal branch has no fallback logging and the fatal branch is unreachable post-exit for the rootElement reason cited in finding 1 (frame_driver.dart:493-496). The stderr fallback at :1280-1288 only runs when `report` itself throws, which the disposed guard prevents. Confirmed by executing a repro: clean exit, then a throwing Timer — nothing printed between 'app exited' and 'DONE', exit code 0. The dispose guard commit (759b8f7) fixed the leaked-Timer issue but left the zone handler with no post-teardown reporting path.

v1.why: Traced: after clean exit, cleanup disposes reporter (run_app.dart:765) and done completes (:1266); the guarded zone persists for app-created Timers/callbacks. A late throw hits the handler (:1268): report() returns at runtime_error_overlay.dart:74 before onLog (:76) so nothing is logged; the stderr fallback (:1280-1288) requires report to throw, which the guard prevents; fatal branch is false (stale non-null rootElement, frame_driver.dart:493-496) and moot anyway (done.isCompleted, :1303). Error vanishes with exit 0 vs root-zone Dart's printed trace + nonzero exit. Guard added by 759b8f7 for a Timer leak with no post-teardown reporting path; no test pins this.
v1.fix_sketch: In the zone handler, detect the post-teardown state (expose RuntimeErrorReporter.isDisposed or check cleanupFuture != null) and write the error + stack to stderr directly — the terminal is already restored at that point so stderr is safe. Keep the disposed guard in report() itself (the Timer-leak fix) untouched.

findings.title: DomRowFactory style/width CSS caches grow without bound for the lifetime of the surface — slow memory leak on style-diverse or color-animating apps
findings.failure_scenario: Long-lived browser session (embed or fleury serve) running an app whose cell styles keep taking new values — e.g. a dashboard pulsing/interpolating truecolor colors per frame, data-driven heatmap colors, or content where CellStyle.linkUri differs per cell run (every distinct URL creates a distinct CellStyle). Every distinct CellStyle ever rendered inserts a permanent entry into _styleCssCache (Map<CellStyle, String>); nothing ever evicts. A session left open for hours accumulates tens of thousands of entries (style object + CSS string each), growing tab memory by MBs with no ceiling; only closing the tab or disposing the surface releases it.
findings.evidence: dom_row_factory.dart:20-21 declare `final Map<CellStyle, String> _styleCssCache = {}` and `_widthCssCache` with no size bound; _styleCssFor (192-201) only ever inserts (`_styleCssCache[style] = css`), never evicts; DomRowFactory is created once per DomGridSurface (dom_grid_surface.dart:24) which lives for the whole session (browser_presentation_host.dart:198). CellStyle includes linkUri (remote_codec.dart style table carries it for v4 peers), so style diversity is unbounded by content, not just by animation. Contrast: InlineImageOverlay's caches are ledger-bounded (inline_image_overlay.dart:82-93) — the style cache has no equivalent policy.

v1.why: dom_row_factory.dart:20-21 declares both maps; _styleCssFor (192-201) and _widthCssFor (146-158) only insert, never evict; grep shows no clear() anywhere. One factory per DomGridSurface (dom_grid_surface.dart:24), one surface per session (browser_presentation_host.dart:198), so entries persist for the tab's life. CellStyle == and hashCode include truecolor foreground/background and linkUri (cell.dart:322-339, 258), making the key space unbounded for color-interpolating or link-diverse content. Slow, real, requires style-diverse app -> P3. (Width cache is effectively bounded — widthCols x cssCellWidth — the style cache is the leak.)
v1.fix_sketch: Bound _styleCssCache with a simple cap-and-reset or LRU (e.g. clear when size exceeds ~4096 entries, mirroring InlineImageOverlay's ledger-bounded policy); optionally exclude linkUri from the cache key by compositing link CSS separately.

findings.title: Failed initial connect leaves the serve page permanently stuck at 'connecting…' with no visible error, no banner, and no retry
findings.failure_scenario: User opens the fleury serve page with a stale/wrong ?token=, or the server is down/restarting (connection refused, or the WebSocket upgrade is rejected). The socket closes before opening -> WireFrameSource start() fails via _failOpen, which calls _teardown(..., banner: false) — explicitly NO reload banner for pre-open failures — and BrowserPresentationHost.attach's catch removes the generated surface/semantic roots. The entrypoint's `unawaited(_run())` never catches the rethrown error, so it becomes an unhandled promise rejection visible only in the console. The page shows a dark screen with the static '#status' element still reading 'connecting…' forever (it only updates when data-fleury-remote-client is set, which happens only after attach succeeds). The user gets zero indication of auth failure vs server-down and no click-to-retry affordance (the mid-session disconnect banner provides exactly that, but this path suppresses it).
findings.evidence: web/remote_client.dart:12-22 (`unawaited(_run())`; `_run` awaits attach with no catch; the 'connected' attribute set only after success); wire_frame_source.dart:67-93 (onclose/onerror before open -> _failOpen) and 114-133 (_failOpen -> _teardown('The fleury session failed to start.', banner: false) -> the failure message is never shown to the user); browser_presentation_host.dart:257-276 (attach catch disposes components incl. removeGeneratedRoots, then rethrows into the uncaught _run future); serve_index_html.dart:28-43 (#status initialized to 'connecting…', updated only via the data-fleury-remote-client MutationObserver).

v1.why: Reachable end-to-end: GET / serves the page with no token check (bin/fleury.dart:636-649 — token gates only the /ws upgrade, _rejectUnauthorizedWebSocket:535), so wrong-token loads reach the client; WS closes pre-open -> onclose (wire_frame_source.dart:67-79) -> _failOpen -> _teardown(banner:false) (:117) -> attach's catch removes generated roots and rethrows (browser_presentation_host.dart:261-275) -> unawaited(_run()) has no catch (remote_client.dart:13), error goes to console only. data-fleury-remote-client is set only on success (:21) and #status 'connecting…' updates only via that attribute's MutationObserver (serve_index_html.dart:34-42). Tests pin no-banner at the source layer (wire_frame_source_test.dart:121,175) but no production caller surfaces the failure. Same for server-down/refused.
v1.fix_sketch: Catch in remote_client.dart _run(): on attach failure set document.body data-fleury-remote-client to a failure message (e.g. 'connection failed — check the token / server, click to retry') so the existing #status observer shows it, and/or render the same click-to-reload banner used for mid-session disconnects (distinguish 401-token vs refused where detectable).

findings.title: Readline kill ring bypasses obscured-field redaction: Ctrl+K/U/W in a password field stores the plaintext in a process-wide buffer yankable into any visible field
findings.failure_scenario: An app gives a password/API-key TextInput (obscureText: true, clipboardPolicy defaulting to redacted) the emacs keymap preset (TextEditingKeymap.emacsSingleLine — an advertised preset). User presses Ctrl+U/Ctrl+K/Ctrl+W in the field: the real secret text is copied verbatim into the static shared TextEditingModel.killRing. Later, Ctrl+Y in ANY other field of the app (chat composer, search box) pastes the secret in plaintext — rendered on screen, possibly submitted, and copyable to the system clipboard from there. The framework deliberately redacts obscured content on the clipboard copy/cut path, so the kill path silently violates the field's confidentiality contract.
findings.evidence: killRange writes the raw substring into the static `killRing` (text_editing.dart:324, :338) with no policy hook. _TextInputState._handleKey routes killToLineEnd/killToLineStart/killWordLeft/yank straight to the controller guarded only by _canEdit (text_input.dart:1268-1291) — no obscureText or clipboardPolicy check — whereas the parallel _copyOrCutSelection path redacts via TextClipboardPolicy.redacted/_redactClipboardText for the same field (text_input.dart:1162-1173), showing redaction is the intended contract for obscured content leaving the field.

v1.why: killRange stores the raw substring in static TextEditingModel.killRing (text_editing.dart:324, :338) with no policy hook; sharing across fields is documented intent (:319-322). _TextInputState._handleKey routes killToLineEnd/killToLineStart/killWordLeft/yank guarded only by _canEdit (text_input.dart:1268-1291) — no obscureText/clipboardPolicy check — while copy/cut redacts obscured fields by default (_effectiveClipboardPolicy :873-875; doc :619; redaction :1162-1173), so the confidentiality contract exists and the kill path bypasses it. Kill bindings are only in the emacs presets (text_keymap.dart:386-403, :451-466; verified zero kill bindings in _defaultSingleLine), and TextInput accepts keymap (:517, :644) — so it requires opting an obscured field into emacs keys (plausible: Ctrl+U is password-prompt muscle memory). Opt-in configuration => P3.
v1.fix_sketch: In the kill action handlers (or controller kill methods), when the field is obscured either perform a plain delete without touching killRing or store a redacted placeholder, mirroring TextClipboardPolicy handling.

findings.title: TerminalOutputRegion/LogRegion: scrollback trim shifts a scrolled-up reader's selection onto different content (ids are list indexes, no re-anchoring)
findings.failure_scenario: LogBuffer at capacity (default 1000) during heavy output. User unfollows (arrow-up) and selects a row to read. Every subsequent captured line trims one head line while the length stays constant, so no count-change/clamp fires and the fixed selection index now denotes the NEXT line's content: the text the user is reading crawls up one row per append, and the selected row silently becomes a different log line while semantic `rowKey`/`rowIndex` report unchanged values (ids are just indexes).
findings.evidence: LogBuffer.add trims the head keeping length == capacity (packages/fleury/lib/src/runtime/output_capture.dart:50-57); buildTerminalOutputLogEntries assigns `id: index` (terminal_output_region.dart:8-16) so identity re-anchoring is impossible; LogRegion.didUpdateWidget has no entries-change selection sync at all (log_region.dart:336-350 -- contrast message_list.dart:301-303 which id-syncs), and ListView only re-clamps on count CHANGE (list_view.dart:486-491), which never fires at constant capacity. Deterministic from code; MessageList solved the same problem with stable ids, this surface cannot.

v1.why: Traced: LogBuffer.add trims head keeping length==capacity (output_capture.dart:50-57); TerminalOutputRegion rebuilds per add (terminal_output_region.dart:84-101) assigning id: index (:11), violating LogEntry.id's documented 'stable identity' (log_region.dart:22-23). Constant count -> _handleCountChange never fires (list_view.dart:486-491); LogRegion.didUpdateWidget has no entries/selection sync (log_region.dart:335-350). Unfollowed reader's fixed selectedIndex and _scrollAnchor sit over shifting content: each append moves the text up one row while semantics rowKey/rowIndex stay constant. Index ids make id-re-anchoring impossible even with a MessageList-style sync.
v1.fix_sketch: Give LogBuffer a monotonic trimmed-count/base offset (total lines ever added) and use base+index as the LogEntry id, then let LogRegion shift selection and anchor down by the trim delta (or add MessageList-style id re-anchoring now that ids are stable).

findings.title: set_value date validation accepts impossible calendar dates and the widget silently normalizes them (e.g. '2026-02-31' → 2026-03-03), violating the advertised reject-by-contract
findings.failure_scenario: Agent calls set_value on a datePicker with '2026-02-31' (or '2026-13-01'). _dateError's regex ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$ passes and DateTime.tryParse (value_schema.dart:149-159) returns a NORMALIZED DateTime (Dart rolls overflow: Feb 31 → Mar 3; month 13 → Jan of next year) instead of rejecting, so validation reports in-domain. The widget's _setDateFromPayload also uses DateTime.tryParse (fleury_widgets/date_picker.dart:162-174) and sets the rolled-over date. The tool returns success and the UI shows 2026-03-03 — a silently different date (or a different YEAR for month overflow) from what the agent set, exactly the silent-remap the schema layer promises to prevent ('an out-of-domain one is rejected by contract, not silently clamped', mcp_server.dart set_value description).
findings.evidence: Dart's DateTime.parse normalizes out-of-range components rather than failing; value_schema.dart:157-159 only checks tryParse != null after the digit-shape regex, never that parsed.year/month/day round-trip to the input. The rest of the validator was explicitly built to 'reject by contract' (commits 2bd9272/d8093ae align validation with widget coercion) — this is the one remaining silent-clamp hole in it.

v1.why: value_schema.dart:157-159 only checks regex shape then DateTime.tryParse != null; verified in Dart: tryParse('2026-02-31') -> 2026-03-03, tryParse('2026-13-01') -> 2027-01-01, both regex-pass — validation reports in-domain. Widget applies the same tryParse and sets the rolled date (date_picker.dart:162-174, _inBounds passes for typical ranges), so the tool returns success with a silently different date/year — contradicting the advertised 'rejected by contract (naming the domain), not silently clamped' (mcp_server.dart:673) and _dateError's own reject-dont-transform intent (value_schema.dart:154-157). No test pins the lenient behavior (value_schema_test.dart:174-192 has no impossible-date case).
v1.fix_sketch: After tryParse in _dateError, round-trip check the components: parsed.year/month/day must equal the regex-captured yyyy/mm/dd ints, else return 'not a real calendar date (e.g. 2026-02-31)'. Validator-side fix suffices since set_value validates before dispatch.

findings.title: `storybook help` command opens the interactive TUI instead of printing usage
findings.failure_scenario: User runs `dart run bin/storybook.dart help` expecting the usage text. _parseCommand maps 'help' to _StorybookCommand.run (line 442) but options.help stays false (only -h/--help set it, lines 371-372), so main() falls through to _runInteractive and takes over the terminal with the full storybook app — the opposite of what a help command should do.
findings.evidence: bin/storybook.dart:435-445 (_parseCommand: `'help' => _StorybookCommand.run`), :349-356 (parse: command consumed as positional, help flag untouched), :9-12 (main only prints usage when options.help is true).

v1.why: Direct read: _parseCommand maps 'help' => _StorybookCommand.run (bin/storybook.dart:442); parse() consumes it as the positional command (start=1, :352-355) and only -h/--help set the help flag (:371-372); main() prints usage only when options.help (:9-12), so `storybook help` falls through to _runInteractive (:16-17) and takes over the terminal. Only existing test pins --help (test/storybook_cli_test.dart:10), not the positional.
v1.fix_sketch: In _parseCommand or parse(), treat 'help' as help=true (print usage and exit 0) instead of mapping it to _StorybookCommand.run.

findings.title: StorybookApp crashes with RangeError when constructed with an empty stories list
findings.failure_scenario: An embedder builds a custom catalog and passes `StorybookApp(stories: const [])` (public constructor parameter). initState computes _selectedIndex=0 then evaluates `widget.stories[_selectedIndex]` -> 'RangeError (length): Invalid value: Valid value range is empty: 0' during mount and the whole app fails to start. Reproduced headlessly at HEAD (stack: storybook_app.dart:92 in _StorybookAppState.initState). The shipped CLI always passes the built-in catalog, so this is embed-only; inconsistent with the rest of the class, which does guard emptiness (_moveStory line 134, initState's own variants branch line 94).
findings.evidence: storybook_app.dart:87-92: `final initialIndex = initialId == null ? 0 : ...; _selectedIndex = initialIndex < 0 ? 0 : initialIndex; _selectedWidgetName = _defaultWidgetName(widget.stories[_selectedIndex]);` — unguarded index into a possibly-empty public-parameter list; constructor at :9-17 accepts any List<Story>.

v1.why: Traced: public constructor accepts stories: const [] (storybook_app.dart:9-17; `stories ?? storybookStories` only substitutes for null; exported via storybook.dart:11). initState with initialStoryId null sets _selectedIndex=0 (:88-91) then indexes widget.stories[_selectedIndex] at :92 -> RangeError on an empty list during mount. Emptiness IS guarded elsewhere (:94 isNotEmpty, :134 count==0), so this is an oversight, not a documented non-empty precondition. Shipped CLI always passes the built-in catalog, so embed-only.
v1.fix_sketch: Either assert(stories == null || stories.isNotEmpty) in the constructor with a clear message, or guard initState/_selectedStory to render an 'empty catalog' placeholder when widget.stories.isEmpty (also covers widget.stories.first.id at :71).

findings.title: fleury shell with non-TTY stdin crashes with an unhandled StdinException on app attach, skipping cleanup and leaving a stale handle/socket
findings.failure_scenario: `fleury shell` is started with stdin redirected (launched from a supervisor/script, `fleury shell < /dev/null`, backgrounded with stdin closed) — `_runShell` only gates on `stdout.hasTerminal` (line 181), so it binds the socket and writes `.fleury/handle`. When an app connects, `_runSession` executes `final originalLine = stdin.lineMode;` (line 319), which throws StdinException ('Inappropriate ioctl for device', empirically verified on a pipe). The throw escapes the root-zone `server.listen((client) async {...})` callback (line 275) as an unhandled async error, killing the shell process without running cleanup() — `.fleury/handle` and `shell.sock` are left behind and the just-connected app dies on transport EOF. (The next `fleury shell` recovers via the stale-handle check, but this launch mode deterministically crashes instead of degrading or erroring cleanly; the terminal itself is untouched since the throw precedes any mode mutation.)
findings.evidence: bin/fleury.dart:181-187 checks only stdout; `_runSession` (298-382) reads/sets `stdin.lineMode`/`echoMode` unguarded at 319-322 before any try/finally protection is established; the `server.listen` callback at 275-290 has no error handling and runs in the root zone, so the rejected callback future is an unhandled error. Getter throw verified empirically: `echo "" | dart lineMode_pipe.dart` → 'StdinException: Error getting terminal line mode, OS Error: Inappropriate ioctl for device, errno = 25'. Contrast posix_driver.dart:324 which guards the same operations behind `_stdinIsTerminal`.

v1.why: Traced and reproduced. Chain: _runShell gates only stdout.hasTerminal (bin/fleury.dart:181), binds socket (213) and writes .fleury/handle (217); on attach, server.listen async callback (275) awaits _runSession (287), which reads stdin.lineMode at 319 before the try/finally at 355. With stdin non-TTY the getter throws StdinException (reproduced: mimic with < /dev/null died exit 255, unhandled in _RootZone — main() has no runZonedGuarded), so cleanup() at 288 never runs; handle file verifiably left behind. Terminal untouched (throw precedes writes at 321-325); next launch self-heals via _checkExistingHandle (1656) → stale → delete (207-211). P3: degenerate launch mode, no corruption, auto-recovery.
v1.fix_sketch: Guard like posix_driver.dart:89/324: in _runShell, if !stdin.hasTerminal either refuse with a clear error next to the stdout check at line 181, or run the session view-only — skip the lineMode/echoMode save/set at 319-322 (wrap in try/catch mirroring the finally's tolerant restores at 373-378) and skip stdin forwarding.

findings.title: Errors thrown in build() are never reported: no stderr log, no banner, no debug-shell error history — the stack trace is lost entirely
findings.failure_scenario: Any widget's build() throws (the single most common user error path). ComponentElement.performRebuild substitutes ErrorWidget in place, so a red panel shows the error's first lines — but runApp wires only owner.onContainedRenderError (layout/paint containment) to the RuntimeErrorReporter and never sets owner.onBuildError, so the report hook at framework.dart:1017 is a no-op. Result: nothing on stderr, no runtime-error banner, nothing in the debug shell's Errors tab (fed exclusively by errorReporter.history), and the stack trace is unrecoverable anywhere (ErrorWidget renders only '⚠ $error'). Every other error class — layout/paint containment, backstop render crashes, event handlers, async zone errors — is reported through the same reporter; build errors alone vanish from all diagnostics. On serve, the agent-facing read_errors debug wire is likewise empty for build failures. Verified by repro: panel painted true, banner false, zero captured stderr lines.
findings.evidence: run_app.dart:584 wires owner.onContainedRenderError = errorReporter.report but no host anywhere assigns owner.onBuildError (grep: only tui_runtime.dart:183 nulls it in dispose; tests set it manually). framework.dart:1013-1020: performRebuild catches the build throw, calls owner?.onBuildError?.call (null) then substitutes errorBuilder — error swallowed except pixels. Debug-shell history provider is set from errorReporter.history only (run_app.dart:586). Reproduced at HEAD with scratchpad/build_error_silent_repro.dart: 'panel painted: true, reporter banner painted: false, stray/stderr lines seen by capture: 0'.

v1.why: Traced at HEAD: runApp uses TuiRuntime() (run_app.dart:448) whose BuildOwner gets only errorBuilder: ErrorWidget.builder (tui_runtime.dart:52-53); repo-wide, no production code assigns onBuildError (only tui_runtime.dart:183 nulls it in dispose). performRebuild's catch (framework.dart:1013-1021) calls the null hook then substitutes ErrorWidget without rethrow, so the error never reaches errorReporter: no stderr (onLog, run_app.dart:579), no banner, no debug-shell history (586), and ErrorWidget shows only '⚠ $error' max 6 lines (basic.dart:246-259) — stack trace lost. run_app.dart:584 wires only onContainedRenderError. P3: error is still visibly surfaced in place; the gap is diagnostics-only.
v1.fix_sketch: Wire `owner.onBuildError = errorReporter.report` alongside onContainedRenderError at run_app.dart:584 (and in the embed/serve host if it builds its own owner). Since report() -> notifyListeners -> scheduleFrame runs mid-build, defer via scheduleMicrotask if scheduling during the build phase is unsafe. Add a runApp-level test: throwing build() -> reporter.history contains the error and stderr got the log line.

items.name: 1) IME caret positioning (composition caret placement)
items.evidence: Commit 8e94c26 (2026-07-15, merged via PR #109 70db065 'launch-terminal-correctness'): the ANSI presenter now repositions the hidden hardware cursor with a final CUP trailer whenever an editable caret exists — packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/ansi_frame_presenter.dart:103 (`_caretPositionSequence(_readCaret?.call(), next.size)`) and :177; TextInput/TextArea publish `focusNode.caretRect` (packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/focus.dart:192), consumed by both ANSI and wire presenters. 102 lines of regression tests added in ansi_frame_presenter_test.dart. docs/implementation/expert-assessment-2026-07-05.md:31 records the item as 'resolved 2026-07-15'. Residual caveat per that doc: native CJK candidate-window/screen-reader behavior remains a release-device check (unprovable headless).

items.name: 2) Inline-mode (non-fullscreen) support — stub / RFC 0016
items.evidence: The tracked pre-freeze item (misleading exported stub + decision) is resolved: commit a887556 (2026-07-12, PR #89) removed the unimplemented `TerminalMode.inline` constant (zero references; selecting it would stomp the real screen). RFC 0016 (docs/rfcs/0016-inline-mode.md:3-6) records status 'Exploration — not scheduled; revisit post-launch' and the pre-freeze disposition as DONE (removal). Grep confirms no TerminalMode.inline anywhere at HEAD. Note: actual inline rendering is deliberately NOT built — a recorded post-launch decision with triggers, not an oversight.

items.name: 3) OSC 8 hyperlink gating design
items.evidence: The conservative ALLOW-LIST design shipped, not the garbage-probe. packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/capabilities.dart:201-234 `detectHyperlinkSupportFromEnvironment`: FLEURY_HYPERLINKS=1/0 override wins; version-checked allow-list (iTerm2 >=3.1 via TERM_PROGRAM_VERSION, VTE >=0.50 via VTE_VERSION>=5000, WezTerm, Ghostty, Kitty, Windows Terminal); allow-listed-under-tmux -> suppressedUnderTmux; unknown terminals default OFF. PR #95 (883ad99, merged 9518d58 2026-07-14) pins Warp & Apple_Terminal OFF the allow-list with a regression test (both only auto-detect URLs, don't honor OSC 8; Warp = open feature request #4194; forced escape verified not honored). The full feature landed via RFC 0017 + PRs #91-#94 (CellStyle.linkUri, wire v4, browser <a> anchors, MarkdownText producers, `fleury diagnose` reason enum). No probe-based emit-by-default was implemented — the only startup cursor probe is the ambiguous-width one (capabilities.dart:38, unrelated).

items.name: 4) Embed overlay (browser-embed RuntimeErrorOverlay entry)
items.evidence: The pre-freeze 'embed overlay' item = the browser embed lacking the runtime-error banner overlay entry (contained runtime errors render on terminal + serve, but not in an embedded app). At HEAD the embed host mounts only the app root entry: packages/fleury_web/lib/src/run_tui_surface.dart:230-256 (`overlayEntries: [rootEntry]`, `debugController: null`, `logBuffer: null`; comment at :232-234 admits the omission). `RuntimeErrorOverlay` (packages/fleury/lib/src/runtime/runtime_error_overlay.dart:111) is mounted only by the native host (run_app.dart:1112-1116); zero references in fleury_web. Explicitly punted by Dan 2026-07-13 ('punt 1-3 for now'). Related but distinct: the embed inline-image overlay itself DOES work (run_tui_surface.dart:718 `_applyImagePlacements`, since d6a749e 2026-07-02, hardened by PR #116 ba01cd1 2026-07-16); its perf-bench follow-up from perf-pass-findings.md:200 also remains open.

items.name: 5) Wire-protocol documentation (serve/remote)
items.evidence: docs/implementation/wire-protocol.md exists and is marked 'Status: Normative' — documents the compatibility policy, frame envelope, versioning, and the explicit 'not a public integration surface' stance. Landed in commit 866e95e (2026-07-12, PR #89 prelaunch-tier1-hardening), which also reconciled the stale FrameType catalog in remote_protocol.dart's header (was missing 0x16 INLINE_IMAGE and 0x1B/0x1C debug frames). Doc is being kept current: updated again by ba01cd1 (2026-07-16, PR #116).

items.name: 6) SIGTSTP/SIGCONT (Ctrl+Z) suspend/resume
items.evidence: PR #73 (merged 9236204, 2026-07-10; commits 563d38e 'make SIGTSTP write-exclusive and single-flight (F7)' + f580478 'harden SIGTSTP gate against a stuck-suspended freeze'). At HEAD packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/posix_driver.dart:615-677 implements `_suspend`/`_resume` with a `_suspended` write-gate (:115) mirroring editor handoff, single-flight, un-gate-and-re-arm if the self-stop didn't take, restore() clearing; CI unit test via seams. SIGCONT repaint landed earlier (aea756f). Design note at posix_driver.dart:33-34/81: Ctrl+Z is consumed via the raw-mode key path -> self-stop (:568); externally-sent SIGTSTP is documented as unsupported because Dart cannot safely watch SIGTSTP/SIGCONT.

items.name: 7) F11 — positional semantic-id churn on the raw wire action path
items.evidence: F11 (from the 2026-07-08 external F1-F27 audit; the audit doc itself was never committed — identified from the engagement memory) = auto semantic ids are positional, so an action sent against a held id can hit a DIFFERENT node after an unkeyed list recycles slots; the MCP path guards this with a per-peer served-snapshot baseline (packages/fleury_mcp/lib/src/mcp_server.dart:149 `_lastServed`, plus stableId:false flagging from #81), but the raw wire path does not. Verified at HEAD: run_app.dart:960-1054 `onSemanticAction` serializes on a per-connection tail (F16 fix) and reads the live root, but invokes `invokeSemanticActionFromElement(id:...)` with NO staleness/fingerprint check — no `_lastServed` equivalent exists in lib/src/remote or run_app.dart. No commit since the 07-14 re-verification touches it (PRs #96-#116 reviewed; #115 is resource bounds, #114 input/teardown). Rated genuinely low: only unkeyed nodes churn, MCP (the primary agent channel) is guarded, apps opt out via Key/Semantics(id:), exposed surface ~= the browser accessible-DOM peer; fix estimated ~25-40 lines cross-layer, MEDIUM risk (hot, wire-gated path).

items.name: 8) README animation example compiles
items.evidence: Commit b522648 (2026-07-14, PR #99 audit-batch4-docs): packages/fleury/README.md animation section rewritten from the nonexistent Flutter-classic API (AnimationController/Tween/AnimatedBuilder — 'Undefined class' for a reader) to the shipped API (AnimationBuilder<T>, Animation<T>..run/.loop, rgbColorLerp). Every snippet is now a literal excerpt of packages/fleury/example/animation_recipes.dart, covered by packages/fleury/test/example/animation_recipes_test.dart (both present at HEAD), so it cannot rot silently. Same commit also fixed the false browser-canvas claim and removed 3 unexported types from the fleury_widgets catalog.

items.name: 9) Perf-gate blind spot (gates measured a non-production config)
items.evidence: Precise finding (3D audit 2026-07-14): gates ran a config narrower than production — alloc/paint gates painted with damage tracking OFF and unstyled text only, serve-semantics profiler timed outside the snapshot, browser wire decode un-gated, FrameDriver telemetry bypassed. (Not literally 'asserts enabled' — gates run under `dart run`, asserts off.) ADDRESSED: (a) the damage-path half — commit a2de01f (PR #97, 2026-07-14) makes alloc-gate call `resetDamageTracking()` and consume bounds each frame mirroring TuiFrameLoop, with an honest re-baseline 9090.0 -> 9186.2 B/frame; verified at HEAD profiling/bin/alloc_gate.dart:206-213. (b) the semantics-wire axis — O(changed) redaction enforced by a deterministic count-based test routed through production code (packages/fleury/test/remote/serve_semantics_redaction_test.dart, PRs #105/#106) after a review proved an alloc gate on that axis unsound (JIT allocation-sinking artifact); #87 also moved alloc-gate under --deterministic to kill JIT bimodality. STILL OPEN: no styled-text/SGR scenario (alloc_gate `_scenario` at profiling/bin/alloc_gate.dart:73-110 is plain Text — the SGR encode-alloc path remains unmeasured; no 'styled' commits since 07-13), browser wire decode still has no gate, and the gate still drives `owner.renderFrame` directly so FrameDriver per-frame telemetry allocs stay outside the window.

gaps.area: Release path never exercised: `dart pub publish` is broken for the core package
gaps.why: Ran `dart pub publish --dry-run` in packages/fleury — it FAILS with a hard validation error: /Users/dan/Coding/fleury/.claude/worktrees/fleury-architecture-review-cb66d3/packages/fleury/lib/src/testing/fleury_tester.dart:38 imports `package:test/test.dart` (used for pkg_test.TestFailure at line 478 and pkg_test.test at line 938) while `test` is only in dev_dependencies. The file's own comment admits the plan ('will become a first-party dep once we split into a dedicated fleury_test package') — the split never happened and nothing gates it. Second blocker in the same run: the `stdio` git dependency (pubspec comment says 'Git dep until published'; pub.dev rejects git deps at upload). The other three publishable packages (fleury_widgets, fleury_web, fleury_mcp) dry-run clean apart from the expected pubspec_overrides hint, and all CHANGELOGs match 0.1.0 — so the whole multi-package launch is blocked solely on fleury core, and none of the 20 audit beats would have caught it because none runs the release command.
gaps.probe: cd packages/fleury && dart pub publish --dry-run → 'Sorry, your package is missing a requirement and can't be published yet.' Follow-ups: split fleury_test (or move `test` to dependencies), publish package:stdio to pub.dev, then add a publish --dry-run step for all four packages to `fleury_dev.dart check` so publishability can't rot again.

gaps.area: Windows is half-shipped with zero testing and no stated support policy; the entire remote/serve/MCP stack is structurally POSIX-only
gaps.why: A real 549-line FFI Windows console driver exists (/Users/dan/Coding/fleury/.claude/worktrees/fleury-architecture-review-cb66d3/packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/windows_driver.dart, selected on Windows by native_driver.dart:27), but CI is a single ubuntu-latest job (.github/workflows/check.yml:10) and the driver's FFI paths self-skip off-Windows (windows_driver.dart:418 returns early when !Platform.isWindows), so the actual console-mode code has never executed in any automated run. Worse: every remote handle binds a Unix domain socket unconditionally — lib/src/remote/spawn.dart:88-90, lib/src/remote/unix_socket_transport.dart:61, bin/fleury.dart:214/570/1661 — and Dart does not support InternetAddressType.unix on Windows, so `fleury serve`, `fleury shell`, and fleury_mcp (app_bridge.dart:504 uses spawnFleuryApp) all die with a raw SocketException on Windows. Yet spawn.dart:239 and bin/fleury.dart:1186 contain deliberate Platform.isWindows accommodations, signalling intent to support. Docs make no platform claim in either direction (the only Windows mention on the whole website is a WT_SESSION row in guides/debugging.md). Users WILL try Windows because the driver ships in the package.
gaps.probe: Attempt `fleury serve --spawn ...` or the getting-started hello world on a Windows box/VM. Follow-ups: publish an explicit support matrix (README + website); add a windows-latest CI job at least compiling/running the driver's unit tests; give the remote handle a TCP-loopback (or named-pipe) fallback on Windows or fail fast with a clear 'serve/mcp not supported on Windows' message instead of a SocketException.

gaps.area: Boot under standard PTY automation crashes with a misdiagnosing error (zero-winsize pty → 'stdout looks piped or redirected')
gaps.why: Found by actually booting the documented hello world under Tcl expect: expect's default spawn leaves the pty winsize at 0x0 (`stty size` → '0 0'), Dart then reports stdout.hasTerminal=false even though isatty(1) is TRUE for the same fd (verified: `sh -c 'test -t 1'` passes inside the same pty, and stdin.hasTerminal=true), and runApp throws 'runApp needs an interactive terminal, but standard output is not a TTY… looks piped or redirected' (/Users/dan/Coding/fleury/.claude/worktrees/fleury-architecture-review-cb66d3/packages/fleury/lib/src/runtime/run_app.dart:431). Setting `stty rows 24 columns 80` before exec makes the identical run paint 'Hello, cells!' and exit cleanly on Ctrl-C. So the framework whose headline pillar is 'agents can drive it' hard-crashes at boot, with a wrong diagnosis, under the most common PTY harness class (Tcl expect, and any CI/agent pty wrapper that skips TIOCSWINSZ; python pexpect escapes only because it defaults to 24x80). The audit's terminal beat fuzzed input parsing but never booted the app under an external PTY harness — a whole failure class (boot-time environment detection) had no coverage.
gaps.probe: expect -c 'spawn dart run bin/app.dart; expect …' with no stty → crash; add `stty rows 24 columns 80` → works. Follow-up: when isatty is true (or stdin.hasTerminal is true) but winsize is 0x0, treat it as a terminal and fall back to env COLUMNS/LINES or 80x24 — or at minimum emit an accurate hint ('your PTY reports a 0x0 window size; set stty rows/columns') instead of the piped-output diagnosis.
